title: Go 1.26 and amqp091-go 1.12 Client Contract · Execution Trace: Unknown Message Validation
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must use amqp091-go APIs, errors, notifications, contexts, and version pins without hiding protocol meaning; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the unknown message validation increment in a reviewed Go client API and lifecycle contract. Learners progress through…
objectives: Treat Delivery fields, properties, headers, and body bytes as untrusted data and validate decoded Go values before domain use.
Classify amqp091-go methods, synchronous errors, notification channels, delivery channels, contexts, and confirmation streams by ownership and completion meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Unknown Message Validation · Theory Unknown Message Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 3083 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate delivered bytes and amqp091 notification ownership without mistaking Go types for validation; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
